excerpt: Opening invocation, recited by the dāʿī al-munṣab (the appointed Summoner), establishes the devotional frame
All praise belongs to Allah, unto whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth; He gives life and causes death
Praise be to Allah, who sent down the Distinguisher (al-Furqān) upon His servant, that it might be a warning unto all the worlds
Praise be to Allah, whose praise the speakers cannot reach, whose bounties the counters cannot enumerate — the doctrine of divine transcendence (tanzīh)
The insistence that Allah transcends predication, that even the noblest human faculties remain incapable of grasping His reality, echoes the via negativa central to Ismaili theology
source_location_hint: Treatise on the Intellect and the Soul

excerpt: The enumeration of the Imam's qualities through the genealogy of Ahl al-Bayt, clustering divine attributes around each Imam—Fāṭimī ʿiṣma, Ḥusaynī courage, Bāqirī science, Ismāʿīlī wisdom
source_work: Rahat al-ʿAql and al-Masabih fi Ithbat al-Imama
source_authority: Chief Ismaili theologian of the Tayyibi tradition
